item.title: Stress Test - Every Link Must Be Explainable in Legal Knowledge
item.description: Stress Test: Every Link Must Be Explainable in Legal Knowledge Seed Rule Being Tested Rule: Every link must be explainable in one sentence — no decorative or keyword-match links.

item.title: Frontier Exploration - Capturing AI Prompt Knowledge
item.description: Frontier Exploration: Capturing AI Prompt Knowledge How should a knowledge base structure knowledge about interacting with AI systems through prompts? The Problem Prompt engineering is a form of procedural knowledge, but it has unique characteristics that existing Seed rules don’t fully address: Exa...

item.title: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Seed Drop this file into any vault to bootstrap a healthy, self-improving knowledge base.

item.title: Anti-Patterns in Knowledge Management
item.description: Anti-Patterns in Knowledge Management Common mistakes that undermine a second brain’s effectiveness.

item.title: CLAUDE
item.description: AI-Assisted Knowledge Management Vault Purpose This vault develops and refines a universal “Seed” — a portable ruleset that lets an AI agent build a high-quality knowledge base on ANY topic from scratch.

item.title: Claude Knowledge Base Setup
item.description: Claude Knowledge Base Setup How to bootstrap an AI-friendly knowledge base for Claude, Codex, Gemini, and other AI agents.

item.title: Confidence Markers
item.description: Confidence Markers Signal the reliability of knowledge in your vault through structured confidence levels. The Problem Not all knowledge is equal.

item.title: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases
item.description: Domain-Specific Knowledge Bases A knowledge base can be customized for any field. Here’s how to adapt for different domains.

item.title: Emergence in Knowledge Graphs
item.description: Emergence in Knowledge Graphs Emergence occurs when a system exhibits properties its individual components don’t have alone.

item.title: Forward References and Planned Knowledge
item.description: Forward References and Planned Knowledge When building a knowledge base, you often need to reference concepts that don’t exist yet — future topics, planned projects, or ideas you’ll develop later.
